leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE - FY2024 underlying operating profit ~GBP 2.5bn on record results (2024 Full Year Results, 27-Feb-2025)

evidence: Rolls-Royce intends to establish India as a strategic 'home market' and is 'looking at more than doubling the size of its workforce to approximately 10,000 people in India and a 10-fold increase in local supply chain sourcing with a long-term goal to position the country as a global hub for its high-tech manufacturing'

Offer for co-development of an AMCA (Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft) engine 'will give India an opportunity to create and own IP for critical combat engine technology that is both customizable and exportable' - reaffirmed 2025-26
IAMPL (50:50 JV with HAL, making jet-engine parts since 2012 in Bengaluru) 'expanded its manufacturing footprint to Hosur in 2024'

More than 4,000 people work across the Rolls-Royce ecosystem in India including 2,800 engineers contributing to global programmes; HAL pact signed for Make-in-India Adour engine parts serving Rolls-Royce's international defence customer base; MT30 marine engine offered with full Transfer of Technology for Indian Navy next-gen destroyers
More than 1,400 Rolls-Royce engines in service across the Indian Armed Forces
india_apac_interest.india_operations: EXISTING footprint (70+ yr HAL relationship, IAMPL JV Bengaluru+Hosur, engineering centre Bengaluru) PLUS large stated NEW expansion ambition (10k workforce, 10x sourcing, AMCA co-development with IP transfer)
india_apac_interest.report_used: Rolls-Royce Holdings Annual Report 2024 (rolls-royce.com PDF) + 2024 Full Year Results + company India statements 2025-26; expansion-ambition quotes are from company/press statements around the AR, not verified as verbatim AR text

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE - FY2024 sales ~GBP 28.3bn, underlying EBIT ~GBP 3.0bn (FY2024 results, 19-Feb-2025)

evidence: Hawk Mk132 trainer built in collaboration with HAL: 'HAL's production line in India where the Hawk aircraft are assembled is a demonstration of the success of Make in India in defence manufacturing'
M777 Ultra Lightweight Howitzer (~$700m, 145 guns): BAE 'down-selected Mahindra as its business partner for the proposed in-country Assembly, Integration & Test (AIT) facility'
Company positioning: 'BAE Systems is proud to be a founding partner of defence manufacturing in India' with increasing collaboration with local industry on Indian Armed Forces modernisation

India is a named international market alongside the company's ~107,000 employees in 40+ countries; adjacent GE-Tejas/AMCA-era programs make India a stated pursuit market for trainers, artillery and naval systems
india_apac_interest.india_operations: EXISTING programme footprint (Hawk-HAL line, M777-Mahindra AIT facility) rather than large owned operations; NEW interest is pipeline-driven (follow-on Hawk, artillery, naval) - Annual Report 2024 itself confirmed published but India-specific AR passages could not be directly extracted
india_apac_interest.report_used: BAE Systems Annual Report 2024 (investors.baesystems.com PDF, published 03-Mar-2025) - India programme evidence from company statements/announcements; AR India text UNVERIFIED verbatim

evidence: Listed Indian subsidiary AstraZeneca Pharma India Ltd 'marks a consistent 32% growth as the company announces its annual results for FY 2024-25' (company press release) after 29% growth in FY2023-24
AZPIL Q1 FY25: 'Reports Strong Q1 Results, Achieving 31% Growth for Quarter ended June 30, 2024'

New Site-Lead, India Operations appointed effective 1-Aug-2024, signalling organisational build-out; India hosts AstraZeneca global capability/technology hubs in Chennai and Bengaluru (widely reported multi-thousand headcount - not verified from AR text in this pass)
India est. 1979, HQ Bengaluru; 800+ commercial employees plus GCC operations
india_apac_interest.india_operations: EXISTING listed arm (AstraZeneca Pharma India, BSE/NSE listed, ~75% parent-owned) growing >30% p.a. + GCC hubs; NEW interest = continued oncology/rare-disease launches and capability-centre expansion
india_apac_interest.report_used: AstraZeneca Annual Report 2024 (astrazeneca.com) + AstraZeneca Pharma India Limited Annual Report 2024-25 (astrazeneca.in PDF)

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE - FY2024 turnover ~GBP 31.4bn, core operating profit ~GBP 9bn (FY2024 results announcement)

evidence: Listed Indian subsidiary GlaxoSmithKline Pharmaceuticals Ltd FY2024-25 'revenue of Rs 3,407 crores with 6% growth... strong volume recovery despite NLEM impact' (India AR 2024-25, titled 'Ahead Together with India')
Shingrix launched in India April 2023 - 'the first vaccine launch across more than seven specialities in the Indian market'; markets outside the US were 56% of 2024 global Shingrix sales

In FY2024-25 'GSK partnered with over 700 clinics and key hospitals across India to transform them into Adult Vaccination Centres (AVx)' - building the adult-immunisation category as a stated India growth driver
india_apac_interest.india_operations: EXISTING listed arm (GlaxoSmithKline Pharmaceuticals Ltd, ~75% parent-owned, Mumbai) with steady single-digit growth; NEW interest concentrated in vaccines (Shingrix) and adult-immunisation category creation rather than new manufacturing capex
india_apac_interest.report_used: GSK Annual Report 2024 (gsk.com PDF) + GlaxoSmithKline Pharmaceuticals Ltd Annual Report 2024-25 (india-pharma.gsk.com PDF)

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE - FY2024 sales ~GBP 1.63bn, adjusted profit before tax ~GBP 260m (Annual Report & Accounts 2024)

evidence: AR 2024: 'A new surfactants plant in Dahej, India is due to be commissioned in 2025, supporting high-growth markets in Asia' - concrete NEW India manufacturing capex
AR 2024: 'Sales in the key Asian markets of China, India and South Korea continued to grow strongly'; 'growth in India was strong, illustrating the continued premiumisation of consumer markets there'
Asia is 25.1% of net sales and the strongest-growing region, with India explicitly one of the investment destinations
india_apac_interest.india_operations: EXISTING Croda India consumer-care business PLUS NEW Dahej surfactants plant (commissioning 2025) - one of the clearest new-investment signals in this cohort
india_apac_interest.report_used: Croda International Annual Report & Accounts 2024 (croda.com PDF)

evidence: Annual Report 2025: 'good growth in Asia as their largest customers in Japan and India outperformed their respective markets' (Clean Air)

Manufacturing footprint in India: Manesar (Haryana) autocatalyst plant, Taloja (Maharashtra) chemicals/catalyst plant, plus Panki operations - 25-millionth Indian autocatalyst milestone previously marked with UK Business Secretary
June-2026: 'Johnson Matthey Proposes Expansion of Chemical Manufacturing Facility at Taloja' (environmental-clearance filing) - incremental NEW India capex

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE - FY2024 turnover EUR 60.8bn, underlying operating profit ~EUR 11.2bn (Annual Report and Accounts 2024)

evidence: India is one of Unilever's core emerging-market growth pillars; >50% of group turnover is from emerging markets with India among the named core growth regions
Hindustan Unilever reaches 9 of 10 Indian households across 50+ brands; HUL EBIT margins exceed 20% and serve as an internal benchmark for group emerging-market margin aspirations
January 2025: HUL 'signed an agreement to acquire the premium actives-led beauty brand Minimalist' - continued NEW investment in premium India categories
Unilever also announced (2025) intent to list its demerged Ice Cream business with Indian operations remaining under HUL structure - India repeatedly cited by group CEO as a top-priority market

india_apac_interest.report_used: Unilever Annual Report and Accounts 2024 (unilever.com PDF) + Q4/FY2024 results announcement + HUL Integrated Annual Report 2024-25

Emerging markets incl. India described as the largest single area in Reckitt's Powerbrand portfolio, 'led by the high-growth markets of China and India'

evidence: FY2024: 'Emerging Markets revenue was up 9.5% (7.6% reported), with strong double-digit growth across the Middle East, India and Latin America' - India explicitly named as a double-digit growth market

evidence: 2024 Annual Report / results materials discuss India primarily as a demand-side driver (steel/aluminium demand growth) rather than an investment destination - no India-specific operational investment plans found in AR search
Rio maintains a commercial/procurement presence in India (Rio Tinto India) but exited its only major Indian project (Bunder diamonds, returned to Madhya Pradesh govt 2017)
Growth capital is directed to Oyu Tolgoi (Mongolia), Simandou (Guinea) and lithium - India absent from the stated growth pipeline

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE - FY2024 (YE Jun-2024) revenue ~GBP 691m, adjusted profit before tax ~GBP 122.6m (Preliminary Results 2024)

evidence: Annual Report 2024: India (Pune) is one of Renishaw's three global manufacturing countries - 'Most of Renishaw's R&D and manufacturing takes place in the UK, and they have other major manufacturing sites in Ireland and India'

AR climate-risk disclosure treats Pune as 'their site with the greatest asset value that currently faces high exposure' to chronic climate risks, producing cables and tool-setting arms - confirming Pune's materiality to group output
'Renishaw recently opened a new technology centre in Bangalore to support their growing customer base in India' - NEW demand-side investment

india_apac_interest.report_used: Renishaw plc Annual Report 2024 'Transforming Tomorrow Together' (renishaw.com PDF) + Preliminary Results 2024

method_notes: Candidate pool: scratchpad/candidates_uk.csv (329 names, 12 focus sectors). All 12 selected leads verified present in the CSV by grep before inclusion.

Requested exemplars NOT in the candidate CSV and therefore excluded: Spirax Group, Weir Group, Diageo, IMI, Smiths Group. Diageo's United Spirits FTA commentary is noted below only as FTA context, not as a lead.

Sector spread of the 12: Aerospace & Defence (2), Pharma & Bulk Drugs (2), Chemicals & Plastics (2), White Goods & Electricals/FMCG (2), Food Processing (1), Medical Devices (1), Specialty Steel & Metals (1), Electronics & Semiconductors (1). Green Energy & Fuels, Auto/EV, Shipbuilding and Textiles buckets in this CSV are dominated by AIM micro-caps/oil juniors with no credible India investment programme, so no lead taken from them.

India-UK FTA (signed May-2025, whisky tariff 150%->75% now, 40% by 2026): most FY2024 annual reports in this cohort were published Feb-Apr 2025 and largely predate or barely post-date signature, so explicit FTA passages were NOT found in the ARs reviewed; the clearest corporate FTA reaction found was Diageo's ('landmark treaty... the largest and most exciting whisky market' - Diageo India CEO), included as calculus context only. FTA relevance to the leads: BAE/Rolls-Royce (defence-procurement climate), Croda/JMAT (chemicals tariff lines), Unilever/Reckitt/ABF (consumer goods inputs).
Profitability figures are from FY results announcements/annual reports and are approximate (rounded); all 12 are solidly profitable at the operating level.

EXISTING vs NEW distinction applied throughout: Unilever(HUL), AstraZeneca Pharma India and GlaxoSmithKline Pharmaceuticals are LISTED Indian arms (existing footprint); NEW-interest signals are Croda Dahej plant, Rolls-Royce 10k-workforce/AMCA offer, AB Mauri north-India yeast plant, HUL Minimalist acquisition, JMAT Taloja expansion, Renishaw Bangalore tech centre.

Evidence gathered via ~14 web searches + 1 direct fetch against annual-report PDFs, IR pages and company statements; quotes are as returned by search extraction of the underlying reports - flagged UNVERIFIED where the sentence could not be tied verbatim to the AR itself (BAE AR India text, Rolls-Royce expansion quotes, Rio Tinto AR India text).
site_access_issues: baesystems.com India country page returned 404 via WebFetch; BAE India evidence sourced from BAE press articles and third-party defence media instead - BAE Annual Report 2024 PDF located but India passages not directly extracted

Large annual-report PDFs (Rio Tinto ~400pp, BAE, Unilever) not parsed page-by-page in this pass; relied on search-indexed extraction of report text, so India sentences for Rio Tinto and BAE are marked UNVERIFIED

Rolls-Royce workforce-expansion (10,000 people / 10x sourcing) quotes come from company statements to Indian media (inkl/Business Standard/Hans India), not confirmed as verbatim Annual Report 2024 text
